SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper clearly states its main claims and supports them with comprehensive experiments on SQuAD (single-model 75.9% F1 vs prior 71.0%, ensemble 80.4% F1) and ablations (Sec. 4.2, Table 2) that isolate encoder/decoder contributions. The model specification (coattention in Sec. 2.2, dynamic decoder and HMN in Sec. 2.3 with Eqs. 1–12) is detailed and implementational choices are reported (Sec. 4.1). Minor issues include lack of statistical significance testing for gains and only qualitative examples for the decoder’s ability to escape local maxima (convergence avg. 2.7 iterations), but these do not materially undermine the empirical claims.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper introduces a novel combination of a coattention encoder (Sec. 2.2) and an iterative/dynamic pointing decoder (Sec. 2.3) using a Highway Maxout Network, which is not merely a rehash of prior single-pass span predictors. The authors situate the work relative to prior co-attention (Lu et al. 2016) and pointer-based QA models (Wang & Jiang; Yu et al.) in Related Work and emphasize the iterative decoder as a non-trivial extension to escape local maxima. Overall the contribution is a notable, clearly-differentiated advance rather than a trivial combination.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (Intro → model with Secs. 2.1–2.3 → experiments Sec. 4 → conclusion) and uses consistent notation with numbered equations (Eqs. 1–12) and referenced figures (Figs. 1–4) to explain architecture components. Implementation details, ablations, and qualitative examples (Appendix A) aid reproducibility and interpretation. A few minor repetitions and dense formulaic sections make parts slightly heavy to parse, but overall presentation is clear and effective.
